D1 Optics L3 Apply Reference

Optical Fiber Acceptance Cone

feed a ray into a fiber and watch it bounce within if it sits inside the acceptance cone

A step-index fiber guides light by repeated total internal reflection at the core-cladding boundary. Only rays inside the acceptance cone defined by the numerical aperture stay confined.

Slide core and cladding indices and the external incidence angle. The gold dashed lines mark the acceptance cone; accepted rays zig-zag down the core, rejected rays bleed out through the cladding.
